Regards,





Phong (Peter) Nguyen.

title: digital integrator
description:
THE ROLE

We are looking for a talented, proactive Video Editor and Content Manager to take Ali’s raw footage and turn it into polished, high-performing content across TikTok, Instagram Reels and YouTube. You will be Ali’s right hand in the content world — someone who understands personal brand storytelling, knows what makes people stop scrolling, and can manage multiple platforms without dropping the ball.

 

This is not a passive editing role. We want someone who brings ideas, spots opportunities, and cares about the growth of the channel as if it were their own.

 

 

WHAT YOU WILL DO

Video Editing

•     Edit raw footage into polished TikTok and Instagram Reels (30–90 seconds)

•     Edit long-form YouTube videos (10–20 minutes) with titles, chapters and flow

•     Cut 3–5 short-form clips from every YouTube video for TikTok and Reels

•     Add subtitles, captions, lower thirds and on-screen text throughout

•     Add background music, sound effects and transitions appropriate to each platform

•     Create eye-catching YouTube thumbnails for every video

 

Content Management

•     Write captions, hooks and post copy in Ali’s voice across all platforms

•     Write YouTube titles, descriptions and tags optimised for search

•     Upload and schedule content across TikTok, Instagram and YouTube

•     Manage a rolling content calendar so nothing is ever late or missed

•     Track basic performance metrics and flag what’s working each week

 

Collaboration

•     Communicate clearly with Ali via WhatsApp or Slack on a daily basis

•     Take briefs, receive raw footage and return edited content within agreed turnaround

•     Proactively suggest content ideas, trending formats and improvements

•     Be responsive, reliable and proactive — not someone who needs chasing

 

 

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Essential Skills — You Must Have These

•  Proven experience editing short-form video for TikTok and/or Instagram Reels

•  Experience editing long-form YouTube content (please include examples)

•  Proficient in CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve or equivalent

•  Strong written English — captions and copy must feel natural and native

•  Reliable high-speed internet and a capable editing computer

•  Self-managed, deadline-driven and highly organised

•  Available for a daily or every-other-day check-in via WhatsApp or Slack

 

Bonus Skills — These Will Make You Stand Out

•  Experience working with personal brands or content creators

•  Understanding of hooks, pattern interrupts and retention techniques on TikTok

•  Ability to create motion graphics or animated text overlays

•  Canva or Photoshop skills for thumbnail creation and social graphics

•  SEO knowledge for YouTube titles and descriptions

•  Experience managing a content calendar and scheduling tools (Buffer, Metricool)

 

This Role Is NOT For You If…

•  You need constant direction and hand-holding

•  You treat deadlines as suggestions

•  You submit work without watching it back first

•  You apply with a generic CV and no portfolio or examples of your work

•  You are not genuinely interested in personal brand content and storytelling

 

 

THE PAID TEST PROJECT — READ THIS CAREFULLY

We do not hire without testing first. This protects both of us.

 

Before any commitment is made, every shortlisted candidate will be given a paid test project. Here is exactly how it works:

 

STEP 1 You apply with your portfolio, a short introduction and your rate.

STEP 2 If shortlisted, Ali will send you a short piece of raw footage (approx. 5–10 minutes).

STEP 3 You will be asked to produce: (a) One 60-second TikTok/Reel edit with captions and (b) One 3-minute YouTube-style cut with subtitles and a thumbnail.

STEP 4 You will have 48 hours to complete and return both edits.

STEP 5 You will be paid $30 USD for completing the test — regardless of whether you are hired.

STEP 6 Ali will review all submissions and the best candidate will be offered the role.

 

We take the test seriously. We expect you to as well. The edit you send us IS your interview.

title: Pet Health Expert and Outreach Veterinarian
description:
Remote veterinary position focused on education, outreach, and veterinary content creation.

Ideal for veterinarians who enjoy client education, communication, and sharing veterinary knowledge with a broader audience.



Position Overview

The Veterinary Education & Outreach Veterinarian helps translate veterinary knowledge into clear, accessible educational content for pet owners while supporting the clinic’s online presence and community outreach. This role manages the clinic’s social media engagement, answers general pet health questions, and promotes Dr. Smith and the clinic’s services through educational content.



The veterinarian will interact with followers by providing general veterinary guidance, sharing educational insights, and helping improve public understanding of veterinary medicine.



This position may be performed remotely; however, the veterinarian must be available during clinic hours from 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST to support online engagement and collaborate with the clinical team.



The veterinarian will also work closely with Dr. Ani to learn aspects of clinical workflow, including documentation, scribing, and reviewing lab reports. This collaboration helps ensure accurate communication of veterinary topics while supporting the clinic’s commitment to high-quality patient care.



This role is designed for a veterinarian who enjoys education, outreach, and public communication while remaining connected to clinical veterinary medicine.



What Makes This Role Unique



* Combine veterinary medicine with education, communication, and outreach.

* Help improve public understanding of animal health through educational content.

* Participate in educational videos, social media content, and veterinary outreach initiatives.

* Collaborate with a clinic focused on preventative care and client education.



Key Responsibilities



Social Media Management



* Manage the clinic’s social media platforms 
, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, etc.).

* Create and schedule engaging educational and promotional content.

* Monitor social media activity and respond to comments and messages professionally.



Community Engagement & Education



* Answer general pet health questions from followers and clients online.

* Provide educational guidance while directing owners to schedule appointments when medical care is needed.

* Maintain a professional, compassionate, and informative tone in online interactions.

* Provide general educational information only; online interactions do not replace veterinary diagnosis or treatment.



Marketing & Brand Promotion



* Promote Dr. Smith’s expertise and the clinic’s services through educational and marketing content.

* Create or assist in developing content (videos, posts, and educational materials) focused on patient care, preventative medicine, and veterinary education.

* Support growth of the clinic’s online presence and reputation in the community.



Clinical Learning & Support



* Train alongside Dr. Ani to understand clinic workflows and medical philosophy.

* Assist with scribing during appointments when needed.

* Help review and communicate lab results and support clinical documentation.



Required Qualifications



* Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM or equivalent).

* Licensed or eligible for veterinary licensure.

* Strong communication and writing skills; fluent in English.

* Ability to clearly explain veterinary topics to pet owners.

* Ability to work remotely while maintaining availability during clinic hours (**8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST**).



Preferred Skills



* Experience with social media, digital content creation, or online education.

* Comfortable appearing in videos or educational media.

* Interest in veterinary education, public communication, and outreach.



Benefits

*Learning and professional growth

*Great team

*9 month pay

*Paid holidays

*Health insurance

title: Level 1 Tech Support Specialist
description:
APPLY HERE: 



NOTE: This position is advertised on other platforms



Level 1 Tech Support Specialist



Location: Europe/Americas/Asia Pacific

Salary Range: AUD: 25-50k

Full time, 40 hours /week, rostered

Work from Anywhere

This role is your launchpad! You'll gain expertise in providing top-notch support, while also having the chance to learn and master new skills beyond the world of level 1 support.



Are you the Tech Support – Level 1 specialist we are looking for?



Are you someone who loves solving problems, helping people, and making technology easier for businesses to use?



Do you enjoy figuring out why something isn’t working and helping someone get back to what they were doing quickly and confidently?



If you enjoy helping people, learning new technology, and working with cloud tools like Google Workspace, GoHighLevel and WordPress, this could be the role for you.



SixFive helps small businesses regain control of their technology so they can focus on their genius zone instead of wrestling with tech. Our mission is to deliver secure, cloud-based systems that support business growth using Google Workspace, WordPress, GoHighLevel and modern automation tools.



We do this with expert knowledge, plain English support, and a genuine commitment to helping our clients succeed.



SixFive is a Tier One Google Partner of more than 14 years.



We’re growing and we need a Tech Support – Level 1 tea
mber to join our support team.



About You



The ideal person for this role enjoys helping people and solving problems.



You’re the type of person who doesn’t ignore an issue - you dig in, figure it out, and follow it through until it’s resolved.



You enjoy learning new tools and technologies, and you take pride in providing great support.



You might be the person we’re looking for if:



You are friendly, calm and helpful when speaking with clients.

You enjoy solving technical problems and troubleshooting issues.

You are extremely organised and able to manage multiple support requests at once.

You communicate clearly and can explain technical things in simple, plain English.

You follow through on commitments and take responsibility for resolving issues.

You are proactive and notice patterns or recurring problems that could be improved.

You pay attention to the small details that make the support experience smooth.

You enjoy learning new technologies and expanding your skill set.

You are comfortable working in a remote team environment.

You are motivated to grow your technical skills and develop your career.



If this sounds like you, keep reading.



About the Role



The Tech Support – Level 1 role reports to the Customer Success Manager and plays a critical role in ensuring our clients receive fast, clear, and helpful technical support.



You will be the first point of contact for many client support requests, helping them solve problems and ensuring they have a positive experience working with SixFive.



This role is responsible for managing support tickets, responding to client queries, and escalating technical issues to the appropriate tea
mbers when necessary.



In this role you will:



Monitor and manage the support ticketing system.

Respond to new support requests within 4 hours of submission.

Provide troubleshooting support for common client issues.

Educate clients to get better value from the tools they already have

Escalate complex technical issues to senior tea
mbers when needed.

Monitor and respond to live chat support requests.

Answer incoming support phone calls.

Maintain clear communication with clients throughout the resolution process.

Document issues and resolutions clearly.

Assist in improving and maintaining Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Work closely with other tea
mbers to improve the support experience.

Your success will be measured by:



Client happiness and satisfaction



Positive client feedback and reviews

Consistent ticket response and resolution times

Continuous improvement in your technical skill set



Core Skills & Experience



We are looking for someone who enjoys working with technology and helping people solve problems.



Helpful experience includes:



Experience providing technical or customer support.

Familiarity with Domain Names, DNS, Google Workspace, WordPress, GoHighLevel and cloud software tools.

Ability to troubleshoot common technical issues.

Strong communication and problem-solving skills.

Ability to stay organised and manage multiple support tasks.

Curiosity and willingness to learn new technologies quickly.



You don’t need to know everything on day one — but you must be motivated to learn and improve.



What You'll Get when you join SixFive



We believe great support comes from people who are supporting themselves.



Here’s what you’ll get when you join SixFive:



A supportive team environment

You’ll work alongside experienced technical professionals who will help you grow your skills.



Opportunities to develop your technical expertise

You’ll gain hands-on experience with Google Workspace, cloud systems, and modern tools.



A fully remote work environment

Work from anywhere while collaborating with a focused, professional team.



A culture focused on learning and improvement

We continuously refine our processes and help our team improve their skills.



Meaningful work

You’ll help business owners solve real problems and get back to running their businesses.



Employment Type



Embrace the remote work life!



This full-time role (40 hours per week) offers the flexibility to work from home where ever you are situated in the world.



How to Apply



Apply Now! 



include the passphrase “Bazinga!” so we know you’ve read the full job description.



We look forward to meeting you.



If you know someone who would be a great fit for this role, please share this opportunity with them.



Thank you for helping us find the right person to join our team.

title: Social Media Manager with Agency Experience
description:
We’re looking for a highly detail-oriented Social Media Manager with agency experience to support our US based agency.

You’ll be supporting a fast-moving team, so this role is for someone who is organized, proactive, creative, and comfortable jumping between content, production, and operations as priorities shift.



This role will involve a mix of responsibilities depending on current needs and priorities, including:



• Creating social media graphics in Canva



• Creating short-form video content and reels using tools like Opus and Canva



• Assisting with writing tasks using AI tools to streamline content creation



• Supporting workflow and production needs to help ensure deadlines are met



• Stepping in on edits or other agency tasks when needed



• Helping with general operational support across the team



•  Bonus points if you have experience editing audio/video



What We’re Looking For



Previous agency experience is required



Strong Canva design skills



Experience creating short-form content for social media



Comfortable using AI tools to support writing and content workflows



Strong organizational skills and ability to manage deadlines



Good written English



Able to follow instructions carefully



Willing to help in different areas of the agency as needs change



Reliable and responsive during work hours



Work Hours



15 hours per week to start



Must be available 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM EST

To Apply

title: Elite DM setter
description:
My Online Fitness Coaching Brand



We are building a high-performance sales team for our rapidly growing online coaching business and are looking for confident, disciplined, and athletic-minded individuals who can communicate professionally and guide potential clients toward life-changing fitness results.



This role is not for passive responders. We are looking for individuals who can lead conversations, build trust quickly, and confidently guide prospects toward booking a call with our coaching team.



The right candidate understands the difference between being assertive and being pushy. You know how to communicate with clarity, confidence, and professionalism while respecting the person on the other side of the conversation.



If you are competitive, trustworthy, and take pride in representing a brand properly, this position offers an opportunity to grow with a serious team.



Responsibilities

 1. Engage with inbound and outbound prospects through social media DMs

 2. Build genuine rapport and qualify potential clients

 3. Clearly communicate the value of our coaching programs

 4. Guide qualified prospects toward booking a consultation call

 5. Maintain professional, confident communication at all times

 6. Represent the brand with integrity and professionalism

 7. Track conversations and booked calls accurately



Who We’re Looking For



We are specifically looking for individuals who are

 Confident communicators who can speak clearly and professionally

 Athletic or fitness-minded and understand the culture of training and performance

 Assertive but respectful able to guide conversations without pressure

 Reliable and disciplined with strong work ethic

 Trustworthy and professional when representing the brand

 Comfortable communicating with high-quality prospects



This role is ideal for someone, I will emphasize again, who takes pride in performance and results, not just showing up.



Compensation



Base Pay

$250 every two weeks



Performance Incentive

3% commission on clients who enroll through appointments booked by the setter to connect with my Onboarding specialist aka Closers for HIgh ticket programs!



High performers who consistently generate quality bookings will have greater earning opportunities as the team grows.



What We Value on the Team!

title: Google Ads And Meta Ads Campaign Specialist
description:
About the Role 

We're looking for a Google & Meta Ads Campaign Specialist to handle the hands-on building, management, and optimisation of client campaigns across both platforms. You'll be working across a portfolio of accounts, executing campaign setups, making day-to-day optimisation calls, managing budgets, and ensuring every account is running cleanly and performing well.

This role suits a detail-oriented digital marketer who enjoys being in the platform daily — building campaigns, analysing performance data, and making the adjustments that move the needle. You'll focus on execution and delivery, keeping campaigns structured, optimised, and aligned with client goals.



What You'll Do

- Build, launch, and manage Google Ads campaigns (Search, Performance Max, Shopping, Display, remarketing).

- Set up and manage Meta Ads campaigns including audiences, placements, budgets, creatives, and A/B testing.

- Monitor campaign performance daily, making optimisation adjustments to improve KPIs (CPA, ROAS, CTR, conversion rates).

- Manage budget pacing and allocation across accounts to ensure spend is on track and efficient.

- Maintain account hygiene — clean structures, correct naming conventions, negative keyword lists, exclusions, and audience management.

- Ensure tracking and attribution are accurate and functioning (GA4, GTM, conversion actions, UTMs).

- Prepare performance reports with clear data summaries, key metrics, and observations.

- Collaborate with the wider team on creative briefing, landing page alignment, and campaign planning.

- Manage eCommerce campaign elements (product feeds, Shopping campaigns, conversion value tracking) where relevant.



What You'll Bring

- 5+ years of hands-on experience managing Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns.

- Proven ability to manage multiple accounts at once while maintaining quality and attention to detail.

- Strong working knowledge of campaign setup, bid strategies, audience targeting, and optimisation levers across both platforms.

- Comfortable reading and interpreting data to make informed optimisation decisions.

- Organised and structured in how you manage your time and workload across competing priorities.

- Clear communicator who can summarise performance and flag issues or opportunities.



Nice-to-Haves

- Experience contributing to campaign strategy, testing roadmaps, or channel planning.

- Familiarity with Looker Studio dashboards or Google Sheets-based reporting.

- eCommerce experience (Shopping, Performance Max, feed management, ROAS/MER tracking).

- Understanding of policy compliance on Google and Meta.

- Exposure to SEO, CRO, or
/SMS marketing for broader context.



Tools You'll Use

- Google Ads, GA4, GTM, Merchant Center

- Meta Ads Manager, Commerce Manager

- Looker Studio, Google Sheets



Engagement

- Part-time (70 hours per month)

- NZ time zone overlap required



What Success Looks Like

- Campaigns are well-built, consistently optimised, and performing to target.

- Budget pacing is accurate with no overspend or underspend surprises.

- Accounts are tidy — clean structures, up-to-date exclusions, and proper tracking in place.

- Performance reports are delivered on time with clear, useful observations.

- Issues are spotted early and flagged or resolved proactively.



If you're a hands-on digital marketer who loves being in the platform, optimising campaigns, and keeping things running like clockwork — we'd love to hear from you.

title: Digital Marketing Specialist
description:

VetShopGroup is one of the largest and longest-running online retailers of pet health products, serving pet owners across Australia and North America.



The business was founded in 1999 by veterinarian Dr Mark Perissinotto and his brother Steven, and today we serve over one million pet owners through our online stores.

title: Legal Virtual Assistant (Lawmatics Experience)
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a Legal Assistant & Client Support Specialist who is both detail-oriented and people-focused. This position blends client coordination, legal administrative work, and systems improvement. You will work directly with the attorney to ensure client matters move smoothly from engagement to completion—while also helping to refine and document our processes as we grow. The right candidate will enjoy creating order, learning independently, and taking initiative.



While the role will start at 15–20 hours per week, it has clear potential to expand into a full- time position with greater responsibility.



Core Responsibilities



Client Coordination & Communication



Serve as the main point of contact for clients across estate planning, real estate, mediation, and business matters.

Conduct 15-minute discovery/intake calls and manage scheduling for client meetings, signings, and mediations.

Guide clients through pre-meeting preparation (forms, portals, document uploads, and next steps).

Communicate clearly and professionally via phone,
, and Slack, maintaining a warm, reassuring tone.

Track progress in Lawmatics and Google Workspace, ensuring deadlines, notes, and follow-ups are current.

Coordinate virtual or in-person signing logistics (notary, witnesses, delivery of executed documents).

Support ongoing client communication and check-ins between meetings.





Process Development & Workflow Management



Collaborate with the attorney to build, refine, and document firm workflows for all practice areas.

Create internal checklists, templates, and automation within Lawmatics.

Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reduce redundancy, and enhance client experience.

Learn independently from written or video resources before seeking clarification, showing initiative and curiosity.

Help organize and maintain the firm’s operations and procedures manual.





Marketing & Outreach Support



Assist with social media posting and engagement on platforms like
, Instagram, and YouTube.

Help prepare and send the firm’s weekly e-newsletter, including light editing, scheduling, and contact management.

Maintain referral partner and contact lists in Lawmatics.

Support the planning of community events (e.g., “Wine & Wills,” presentations, and expos).

Track basic marketing metrics (registrations, event attendance, new client leads).





(*Note: Most physical office tasks, such as scanning or binder assembly, will be handled by another tea
mber.)



Key Skills & Qualities



Strong written and verbal communication skills—clear, confident, and client-friendly.

Highly organized with meticulous attention to detail.

Tech-savvy and comfortable using Lawmatics, Google Drive, Docs, and Sheets.

Independent learner: capable of finding and applying information from provided resources.

Process-oriented: enjoys improving systems and building structure.

Professional and compassionate demeanor, especially when working with families or sensitive matters.

Reliable, self-motivated, and adaptable in a small firm environment.





Preferred Experience



2+ years of experience supporting a law firm

Background in real estate, estate planning, or mediation/family law preferred.

Experience coordinating files, clients, or project workflows.

Experience in Lawmatics

Notary Public in NY (or willingness to obtain) is a plus.

Comfort with social media platforms and light marketing tasks.

title: digital organisation specialist
description:
JOB DESCRIPTION:

I am Ali Rihani — founder of Emerald Sands, based in Dubai. I run a consulting and deal brokerage business, I create content on TikTok and I am building a personal brand called RIHANI.

My digital life is a mess. Files everywhere. Multiple clouds. No system. No logic. I need one person to come in, clean everything up and build a filing structure I can actually follow going forward.

This is not a creative role. This is not a tech role. This is a systems and organisation role. I need someone who has done exactly this before and has a method they follow — not someone who will figure it out on my time.



THE PROJECT — WHAT YOU WILL DO:



Audit my current digital storage — Google Drive, iCloud and any other cloud platforms I am currently using. Understand what exists, where it lives and what the current state of chaos looks like.

Build a clean master folder structure from scratch across six top-level categories — Brand, Business, Content, Clients, Course and Personal. Inside each category, logical sub-folders that make sense for how I actually work.

Migrate all existing files into the new structure. Nothing lost. Everything in the right place. Old duplicates removed.

Create a simple file naming convention I can follow without thinking. Date first. Plain English description. Version number. Consistent across everything.

Deliver one printed reference guide — a single page that tells me where every type of file goes, what to call it and what to do with it when a project is complete.





THE THREE SKILLS YOU MUST HAVE:



Google Drive organisation and migration — you have done this before for a real client, not just your own files. You can look at a chaotic Drive and have it clean, logical and searchable within a few days.

File naming conventions and folder architecture — you have a methodology you follow, not one you invent as you go. You can explain your system clearly before you start work.

Cloud consolidation experience — you have worked across multiple cloud platforms simultaneously and successfully pulled scattered files into one unified structure without losing anything.





THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:



You have never organised someone else's digital files before

You need detailed instructions for every step

You treat a deadline as a suggestion

You are looking for ongoing work — this is a defined project with a clear end point





HOW TO APPLY:

Your application must include three things. Applications missing any of these will not be reviewed.



One example of a folder structure or filing system you have built for a previous client — screenshot or brief description.

Your file naming convention — tell me in two sentences exactly how you name files and why.

Your fixed project rate in USD for approximately 15 hours of work.





Emerald Sands — Founded by Ali Rihani — Dubai, UAE

Local Eyes for Global Minds

title: Head of Design & Social Media (Graphic Design + Social Media Strategy)
description:
Head of Design & Social Media (Graphic Design + Social Media Strategy)



We are looking for an exceptional Graphic Designer & Social Media Strategist to lead the visual and creative direction across our brand.



You will be responsible for all graphics, social media content, landing pages, workbooks and marketing assets, while also helping plan and execute social media strategies that grow the brand and drive results.



This is not a beginner role. We are looking for someone with 5+ years experience who can take full ownership of design and social content.



Responsibilities

? Lead and manage all brand graphics and design assets

? Plan and execute social media strategies and content calendars

? Create high-performing social media content (Reels covers, carousels, posts etc.)

? Design landing pages, funnels and digital marketing assets

? Create workbooks, presentations, event materials and campaign graphics

? Maintain strong brand consistency across all platforms

? Work closely with the marketing team to execute campaigns quickly



Requirements

? 5+ years experience in graphic design and social media strategy

? Outstanding design skills — no basic or template-level designers

? Extremely high attention to detail

? Able to produce clean, premium, high-converting visuals

? Data-focused and understands what content performs

? Strong English communication skills

? Able to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment



Bonus

? Experience creating Meta Ads creatives that convert

? Experience with funnels, landing pages and digital marketing campaigns



How to Apply

To apply for this role, please submit:

? Your CV / Resume

? Examples of past work or portfolio (social media, graphics, landing pages etc.)

? A short 60-second video explaining why you would be the right fit for this role

Please note: We will not review generic applications.

We want to see your personality, your thinking and why you specifically want this role.



We are looking for someone who thinks strategically, executes quickly and produces outstanding design that stands out.

If you are creative, strategic and obsessed with great design, we want to hear from you.

title: Senior Accountant – Multi-Unit Operations
description:
Who we need:

We are hiring a sharp, disciplined accounting professional to help bring structure and control to a fast-moving multi-unit restaurant business. This role is not just bookkeeping. We need someone who can create weekly accountability, strengthen operational control, enforce internal discipline, and provide clear financial visibility to ownership and work with other Senior accounting professionals and CPAs 



Key responsibilities:

 • Prepare and review weekly sales, cash, labor, and prime cost reporting

 • Monitor P&Ls, payroll, invoice flow, and vendor balances

 • Maintain strong internal controls over cash, expenses, and reporting

 • Identify errors, missing invoices, unusual spending, and operational leaks

What we want:

 • Strong accounting background

 • Organized, detail-driven, and tough on follow-through

 • Able to spot problems fast and fix them.

 • Understands both accounting and operations Will be helpful.

title: Customer Support Representative
description:
Smarcomms is hiring a Remote Customer Support Representative who is an expert in all things social media.



We are looking for a confident and assertive customer support rep who can think on their feet.



If you can problem solve and make your own decisions then this is the job for you.



If you are more introverted, and prefer to follow a set process, always need to ask a manager. This is not for you.



The role will be full-time for $500 per month. If you can work a US EST shift that would give your application extra bonus points.



Smarcomms is a social media management company providing small business owners with 'done for you' social media content and real organic growth for one low monthly fee. We’re a small and highly effective remote team of mainly independent contractors – you’ll work independently from wherever you want.



Benefits of this role:

Here’s why this is an awesome job.



- This is a role where you will have a direct impact on our customers' experience and how much they get out of our service. Our customers require in-depth replies that go the extra mile to solve their issues, not rushed answers with no thought.

- You'll work remotely. Work from anywhere and enjoy the freedom of working where you want.

- You'll work Monday-Friday.

- You'll get paid $500 per month.



The ideal candidate:

We’re open to hiring people with different backgrounds, experiences, and skills. If you enjoy customer service, have a strong understanding of social media and are fairly tech savvy then you are likely to be a good fit.



There are some attributes that are guaranteed to make you awesome at this:



- Empathy: sometimes clients get frustrated and it’s our job to understand and find a solution.

- You’re a self-starter, you like to set your own direction and run with it. You don’t need constant check-ins to get things done.

- You’re good at expressing ideas in clear and concise writing. The tone of our company is very personal, and being able to write simply is a must.

- You are tech-savvy. You’re able to quickly learn new software and figure out problems as they arise.

- You’re reliable. This is a remote position and nobody is going to look over your shoulder. Show up when you say you will do what you say you’ll do.

- You're able to think for yourself and not always need to ask a manager.

- Finally, you’re proactive about moving the company forward. When you notice areas for improvement you take the initiative to create change.



Salary and compensation

$500/month



Apply here:

title: Owner-Operator & Driver Recruiter
description:
Position Overview



The Owner-Operator and Driver Recruiter is responsible for identifying, recruiting, and onboarding truck drivers and owner-operators who can haul freight for the company’s freight brokerage operations throughout the United States.



This role focuses on building a reliable and scalable network of drivers capable of moving freight across multiple freight markets including ports, regional freight corridors, and national shipping lanes.



The recruiter will proactively identify independent drivers, small trucking companies, and owner-operators interested in hauling loads for the brokerage. The recruiter will maintain a database of available drivers and ensure the operations team has access to qualified transportation capacity when freight opportunities arise.



This position plays a critical role in ensuring the brokerage has access to the transportation capacity needed to support customer shipments.



?



Purpose of the Role



The primary purpose of the Driver Recruiter is to continuously build and maintain a network of qualified drivers and owner-operators who are capable of hauling freight secured by the brokerage’s sales team.



By maintaining a reliable pool of drivers, the recruiter helps ensure the company can fulfill freight commitments quickly and efficiently.



?



Key Responsibilities



The Owner-Operator and Driver Recruiter will be responsible for sourcing, contacting, and onboarding truck drivers who are capable of hauling freight in the United States.



Responsibilities include:



• Identifying and contacting independent truck drivers and owner-operators

• Recruiting small trucking companies interested in hauling freight

• Posting driver recruitment advertisements on driver job boards and trucking forums

• Contacting drivers through phone,
, and online platforms

• Maintaining a database of qualified drivers and trucking companies

• Assisting with the onboarding process for drivers who join the network

• Maintaining ongoing communication with drivers to confirm availability

• Tracking recruitment activity using the company’s operational tools



The recruiter will work closely with operations to ensure that freight loads can be matched with available drivers.



?



Driver Types Recruited



The recruiter will primarily focus on recruiting:



• Owner-operators with their own trucks

• Independent truck drivers

• Small trucking companies with multiple trucks

• Regional carriers capable of handling specific freight lanes



These drivers may handle:



• Truckload freight (FTL)

• Port drayage freight

• Regional freight lanes

• Long-haul freight



?



Recruitment Sources



Drivers may be recruited through several channels, including:



• Trucking
 groups

• Driver job boards

• Owner-operator forums

• Trucking communities and online networks

• Direct outreach to small trucking companies



The recruiter must be comfortable proactively reaching out to drivers and carriers to introduce the company and explain available freight opportunities.



?



Operational Tracking



All recruitment activity must be documented using the company’s operational tools.



The recruiter will track:



• Drivers contacted

• Driver contact information

• Equipment type (truck and trailer type)

• Freight lanes the driver prefers

• Availability and geographic coverage



Maintaining accurate records ensures the operations team can quickly identify drivers capable of hauling specific loads.



?



Weekly Performance Expectations



Typical weekly performance targets include:



• Contacting 30–50 drivers or trucking companies

• Recruiting 3–5 qualified owner-operators into the company network

• Expanding the driver database continuously



Over time, the recruiter will build a large and reliable network of drivers capable of covering multiple freight markets.



?



Geographic Freight Markets



The brokerage may operate across multiple U.S. freight markets. Initial operations may focus on freight hubs such as:



• Savannah

• Charleston

• Los Angeles

• Houston

• New York / New Jersey



Drivers may operate in regional lanes or long-haul routes depending on their equipment and operating preferences.



?



Required Skills



Candidates for this role should have:



• Strong communication skills

• Experience recruiting contractors or drivers (preferred)

• Ability to organize and track driver information

• Comfort contacting drivers and trucking companies



Helpful experience includes:



• Trucking industry knowledge

• Logistics industry experience

• Driver recruitment experience



?



Contribution to Company Growth



The Owner-Operator and Driver Recruiter is essential to ensuring that freight secured by the sales team can be successfully transported.



By continuously expanding the driver network, this role ensures the brokerage has the capacity needed to move freight efficiently and maintain reliable service for shipping customers.



A strong driver network allows the company to grow into new freight markets while maintaining a small and efficient operational structure.

title: Freight Sales Representative – Freight Brokerage #2
description:
Job Overview



We are a growing freight brokerage focused on moving freight throughout the United States. Our current markets include freight moving through major logistics hubs such as Savannah, Charleston, Los Angeles, Houston, and other high-volume freight locations.



We are looking for experienced Freight Sales Representatives who are capable of building their own book of business and generating freight opportunities through their experience and industry knowledge.



This role is ideal for someone with 2–3 years of freight brokerage or logistics sales experience who understands the freight industry and can begin prospecting and securing customers quickly.



Candidates must be comfortable identifying shipping companies, contacting decision-makers, and securing freight lanes for our brokerage.



This is a performance-driven position with strong commission potential and long-term income from customers you bring into the company.



?



Compensation



Base Salary

$300 per month



Commission

10% of gross revenue generated from customers you bring to the company.



Important:

This commission is lifetime commission.



Once you bring a customer to the company, you continue receiving the 10% commission on that account for as long as the account produces freight revenue.



This structure allows experienced sales professionals to build long-term income as their book of business grows.



?



Required Experience



Applicants must have 2–3 years of experience in the freight or logistics industry, including experience in one or more of the following:



• Freight brokerage sales

• Third-party logistics (3PL) sales

• Truckload freight sales

• Logistics account executive roles

• Transportation sales

• Drayage or port freight coordination



Candidates should understand:



• Freight lanes

• Truckload and LTL freight

• Shipping volumes

• Logistics pricing and quoting

• Import/export freight movement



This experience should allow the candidate to identify potential customers independently and begin generating freight opportunities quickly.



?



Key Responsibilities



• Identify companies that ship freight regularly

• Prospect shipping companies through phone calls,
 outreach, and LinkedIn

• Build and maintain your own book of business

• Secure freight lanes and shipping agreements

• Generate freight quotes and proposals

• Build relationships with shipping managers and logistics coordinators

• Coordinate with operations once freight is secured



Sales representatives must be comfortable working independently to identify freight opportunities and develop new business.



?



Sales Process Tracking



All sales activity must be tracked using the company’s operational tools.



Sales representatives will be expected to maintain accurate records of:



• Companies contacted

• Sales pipeline stages

• Freight opportunities

• Customer communication history

• Active freight lanes



Because of your experience, you should be able to quickly manage your sales pipeline and move prospects through the sales process efficiently.



?



Geographic Freight Markets



While our initial operations may focus on markets such as Savannah, our goal is to maintain a small but highly effective freight operation that can pursue opportunities across multiple freight markets.



Sales representatives must be comfortable identifying opportunities in locations such as:



• Savannah

• Charleston

• Los Angeles

• Houston

• New York / New Jersey

• Other major freight hubs



Candidates should be comfortable pursuing freight opportunities in different geographic markets when necessary.



?



Daily Expectations



Typical daily activity may include:



• 50 outbound prospect calls

• 50 outbound
s

• 10 freight quote opportunities

• Building relationships with shipping companies



?



Ideal Candidate



The ideal candidate:



• Has freight brokerage experience

• Understands the logistics industry

• Is comfortable making cold calls

• Can generate freight opportunities independently

• Wants to build long-term commission income



?



Application Instructions



To apply, please include:

 1. A brief description of your freight experience

 2. The type of freight you have worked with

 3. Your experience in freight sales or logistics sales



To confirm that you read this entire job post, please begin your application with the words:



FREIGHT TEAM

title: Full-Stack WordPress Developer and Designer for our Web Design Agency
description:
Company Overview:

We are a stable and longer term Web Design & Digital Agency located in the United States. With a comprehensive suite of services, we aim to save small businesses both time and money. We have over a decade of experience and have garnered stellar reviews from our satisfied clientele. We are on a mission to improve online presence for businesses and are looking for a dedicated individual to join our team and contribute to our vision.



Job Description:

We are looking for a versatile Full-Stack WordPress Developer and Designer to join our team. This role requires a balance of skills in both web development and design, with a focus on creating and maintaining high-quality small business websites. You will be responsible for not only designing websites using Elementor but also handling the technical aspects of WordPress development, including plugin troubleshooting, website maintenance, and basic hosting management on Plesk. About 40% of your time will be spent in Web Design, 40% in Development/Technical, and 20% in administrative tasks related to WordPress web design. You'll be working directly with clients in our Helpdesk, so proven customer service and English communication skills are a must.



This is a long-term opportunity to work with a 23 year old web design agency.



You need to have an eye for design and have already established the skills to produce beautiful design work for small business websites and can handle publishing and working with Elementor well to be good at this job.



You must have great organizational skills and decision making abilities. As you will be working with our US design team, you need to have excellent English writing, spelling, and punctuation is a must since you will be working in customer websites.



Our focus is on a quality candidate is your ability, passion, and desire to always improve. We are a fast paced company with a professional and positive environment where our teams work together, roll up their sleeves and pitch in with whatever is needed. You will prioritize your workload to meet deadlines and work objectives.



There is a large opportunity for growth and role expansion as we are a well established digital marketing company and looking to expand our small but talented team of designers and developers.



Key Responsibilities:



• Design and develop WordPress websites, ensuring both aesthetic appeal and technical functionality.

• Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to WordPress plugins and website performance.

• Manage WordPress hosting environments, including basic tasks on Plesk.

• Collaborate with clients and tea
mbers to deliver high-quality web solutions.

• Contribute to the creation and improvement of SOPs, and manage administrative tasks related to web design and development.



Qualifications:



• 5+ years of professional experience in WordPress development and design.

• Proficiency in Elementor and a strong portfolio showcasing your design skills.

• Solid understanding of web development principles, including experience with troubleshooting plugins and website issues.

• Familiarity with web hosting environments, preferably Plesk.

• Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail, and a team-player attitude.

• Fluent in English with strong communication skills.



Benefits:



• Work-life balance with a 35-hour work week.

• Flexible payment schedule and competitive compensation.

• Opportunities for growth and professional development.

• Bonuses, Paid Time Off, Company Holidays, 13th Month.

• Guaranteed raise schedule on base pay.



Ideally, you are:

- Fluent in English and can type quickly

- Comfortable and proficient communicating with American co-workers.

- Proficient with
, Google Workspace, and Slack is necessary.

- Highly organized, timely & reliable.

- Available to work primarily in our timezone, which is EST.



Application Instructions:



Submit your resume and portfolio through OLJ. Please include the phrase "I AM FULL-STACK" in your proposal to confirm you have read the entire job description.

title: Lead Research & Calling Virtual Assistant (US Businesses)
description:
We are looking for reliable Virtual Assistants to research businesses and collect information from business owners over the phone.

This is not a sales job. Your role is to research businesses, call them, and collect basic insurance-related information that helps our team generate leads.

The first part of each shift will be lead research, and the second part will be outbound calls to verify business information and collect details.

All scripts, instructions, and systems will be provided during training.



Responsibilities

• Research businesses in assigned industries and cities

• Add leads to our lead tracking system

• Call businesses to verify information

• Collect insurance-related information from the business owner or manager

• Update lead status and notes in our system

• Follow a simple script when calling

Daily Tasks

• Research new businesses in assigned categories

• Make outbound calls to business owners

• Record call results in our system

• Schedule appointments for our manager

Typical call volume: 80–120 calls per day

Requirements

• Clear English speaking ability

• Comfortable speaking on the phone

• Reliable internet connection

• Quiet work environment

• Basic computer skills

• Ability to follow scripts and instructions



Experience with cold calling, appointment setting, or lead generation is a plus but not required.



Work Schedule

Two types of roles are available

.

Schedule Option 1

Monday – Friday

10 AM – 3 PM Eastern Time



Schedule Option 2

Wednesday – Sunday

11 AM – 3 PM Eastern Time



Compensation

• $1.50 per hour base pay

Additional performance bonuses are paid for completed leads:

• Basic Lead: $1 per lead

• Qualified Lead: $3 per lead

• High Intent Lead: $6 per lead

These bonuses are paid along with the hourly rate.

Companies typically purchase 50–100 leads at a time, creating ongoing work for our team.

Payments are sent weekly through Wise.



Training

Selected applicants will attend a live training session where we will cover:

• Our lead research process

• The calling script

• How to log leads correctly

• How to use our tracking system



To Apply: Complete the form.

title: HubSpot PM/Specialist (Operations + CRM Support)
description:

This role is primarily project management and operations, but the candidate must already be familiar with HubSpot and comfortable performing basic CRM tasks when needed.



You will work directly with the founder to help manage client implementations, keep projects organized, and assist with light HubSpot administration.

---

Project Management (Primary)

Convert meeting recordings and notes into actionable tasks



Track deadlines and follow up on deliverables



Maintain documentation and project SOPs

HubSpot Administration (Required Knowledge)



Navigate and manage records in HubSpot CRM



Create and manage lists



Update properties and contact/company records

Experience creating lists or reports

Experience with task management tools (ClickUp, Asana, Monday, etc.)



Strong communication and documentation skills.



Clearly speaks English. 



Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

---

Preferred Experience

Tools



HubSpot

ClickUp

title: Customer Support Specialist
description:
IMPORTANT NOTE: EXPERIENCE SUPPORTING EDUCATIONAL TECHNOLOGY PLATFORMS IS HIGHLY PREFERRED.



STEPS TO APPLY:

About the Role

title: Non-voice Business Development Representative / Lead Generation Specialist (100% WFH)
description:
We’re looking for a motivated and results-driven BDR / Lead Generation Specialist to help grow our sales pipeline and expand our customer base. As a BDR, you’ll be on the front lines of our sales efforts, identifying potential clients, generating leads, and nurturing opportunities to connect them with our solutions.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Proactively research and identify potential customers in target markets.

2. Reach out to prospects via
s, LinkedIn, and other communication channels.

3. Qualify leads to assess fit and interest, ensuring alignment with our services.

4. Schedule meetings or product demos between qualified leads and Sales Development Representatives.

5. Maintain accurate records of lead interactions and update CRM systems accordingly.

6. Collaborate with the marketing and sales teams to execute campaigns and optimize lead generation strategies.

7. Stay up-to-date on industry trends and our product/service offerings to effectively communicate value propositions.



Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

- 2 - 3 years of experience in sales, business development, or a similar role (entry-level candidates with strong communication skills and a hunger to learn are also encouraged to apply).

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

- Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build rapport quickly.

- Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and eager to exceed targets.

- Proficiency in extracting data from LinkedIn Sales Navigator and other professional networks

- Must have a well-optimized LinkedIn account with atleast a minimum of 300 connections that can be used for prospecting

- Familiarity with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, PipeDrive) is a plus.

- Comfortable with cold outreach and prospecting.



Who we are:

Acadium started with the belief that anyone, anywhere, should be able to get the skills and experience they need to start their career without barriers like price, schedule, or location getting in their way. Acadium gives anyone the tools, support, and mentorship they need to launch their dream career. Acadium is the only platform that provides real work experience, accessible to everyone. Acadium began in 2017 with a team of 3 co-founders: Richard Shaw, Marco Tundo, and Moe Abbas. Since then, they’ve attracted more than 120,000 student members and over 50,000 businesses to connect through the apprenticeship marketplace. Our team is growing and looking for people who are motivated, intelligent, and committed to making education accessible to join our team.



What we value:

Integrity - Be trustworthy in your actions

Care - Treat your teammates and our community as you would want to be treated

Curiosity - Ask questions. Always state your assumptions

Tenacity - Persevere when times are tough

Discipline - Be consistent in your actions

Ambition - Aspire to become better every day

Service -Serve your community to the best of your abilities.



Interested applicants may send updated CVs and LinkedIn URLs to the
 provided below.

 Subject: [Adjective that describes you] B2B Business Development Representative

**Note: Only applicants who submit their resumes and LinkedIn URL to 
 will be evaluated.

title: Condo Property Manager (Remote – Full-Time, Independent Contractor)
description:
About Superior Property Group:



At Superior Property Group, we have been investing in rental properties for over 20 years. After experiencing dissatisfaction with traditional property management companies, we began managing our own properties. We developed our systems from the ground up, with a deep understanding of rental real estate investment. Our processes and systems are crafted from an investor’s perspective, not just a property manager’s, ensuring the highest standards for the properties we oversee.



We manage each property as if it were our own, applying the same rigor to tenant selection, property maintenance, and operational efficiency. From handling tenants to routine maintenance, we are committed to the success of each property we manage, as deeply as we are to our own investments.



We are a remote team spread across the United States, Mexico, the Philippines, and Uruguay, and we take pride in our asynchronous work culture. With a flexible work-life balance, we empower our tea
mbers to work in a way that fits their personal schedules while staying connected to the team's overall goals.



About the Role:



We are seeking a highly skilled and customer-focused Condo Property Manager. The ideal candidate will have 2-3 years of residential property management experience. This role requires a strong ability to manage property operations remotely while maintaining exceptional customer service.



NOTE: This is an Independent Contractor Position.



Scope of Work:



The Condo Property Manager will ensure smooth operations and excellent support for residents. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:



Property & Project Management: Oversee property operations, project management, and owner relations. Ensure smooth day-to-day operations by coordinating with onsite staff and vendors.



Customer Service: Provide top-notch, proactive customer service to condominium owners, addressing inquiries and concerns, and ensuring resident satisfaction from a remote setting.



Vendor Management: Collaborate with vendors, solicit bids for projects, and provide the Board of Directors with recommendations on service providers. Manage relationships to ensure service quality and cost-effectiveness.



Document Management: Understand and effectively articulate the condominium’s governing documents (e.g., declarations, bylaws, rules, and regulations) and ensure compliance across operations.



Board Collaboration: Provide administrative, operational, and managerial advice to the Board of Directors. Act as the main point of contact, reporting regularly on community operations and finances.



Financial Reporting & Risk Management: Review financial statements and advise the Board on financial status, budgeting, investment strategies, and any budget variances. Provide risk management recommendations to ensure the community's long-term financial health.



Board Meetings: Attend and support virtual Board meetings, providing updates and recommendations for ongoing property management needs and projects.



Other Duties: Handle additional responsibilities as needed to maintain the smooth operation of the condominium community.



Qualifications:



2-3 years of residential property management experience

Strong decision-making and leadership abilities, with the capacity to manage resources remotely.

Demonstrated ability to provide exceptional customer service, balancing the needs of residents, boards, and vendors.

Knowledge of community associations, non-profit organizations, or related business experience.

Proficiency in reading and interpreting federal/state laws, as well as association documents (CC&Rs, Bylaws, etc.).

Familiarity with hosting meetings, property management practices, accounting procedures, and financial management.

Exceptional English communication skills (written and verbal) with a strong focus on clarity and accuracy.

Excellent customer service and interpersonal communication abilities.

Ability to multitask and manage time effectively in a dynamic environment.

Proficiency in using office software (e.g., Google Workspace, Notion, Asana).

Attention to Detail: Highly organized, with a proactive approach to follow-up and task management.



Nice-to-Haves:



Previous experience working in a start-up or building processes from the ground up.



Availability:



Schedule: Monday to Friday, totaling 40 hours per week.

Working Hours: Standard hours are 8 AM to 5 PM CST, but we offer flexibility to adjust based on your schedule and work needs. We value work-life balance and understand that different time zones may require adjustments, as long as key responsibilities are met and team communication is maintained.

Quiet Workspace: A quiet office environment is required for making and receiving calls.



Technical Requirements:

Minimum internet speed of 10 Mbps (15 Mbps or higher preferred).

A reliable desktop computer or fast laptop running Windows 8 or a more recent version of Windows, or an iOS device. The system should have at least 8 GB of RAM and 256 GB of available hard drive space.



Practicals:



You’ll report to the Vice President of Condominiums and New Developments

Team: Condos

Location: Anywhere in the World

Start date: As soon as possible



Application Process:



(Async) Profile review

Interview with Chief People Operations Officer

Interview with the Vice President of Condominiums and New Developments

Offer



Benefits:



Competitive pay + performance bonuses

Long-term, full-time remote work with a stable U.S. company

Career growth and advancement opportunities

Supportive and team-focused work culture

Paid holidays and PTO



How to Apply – Read Carefully!



Please submit all applications and CVs in English, as English is the standard language at Superior Property Group.



To be considered, you must submit all 3 items below. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



Resume 



Notion “About Me” Page

Create a page in Notion about yourself and share the public link

This helps us see your creativity, organization, and tech comfort



Video Introduction (Max 2 minutes)

Share a link (YouTube, Loom, etc.) introducing yourself and answering:

Who are you, and what’s your background?

What experience do you have in property or HOA management?

Why are you excited to join Superior Property Group?



Important: Please ensure your Notion and video links are accessible. Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.



Join us in this fully remote role and help streamline our Condo operations from anywhere in the world!

title: Remote recruiter
description:
Hi there! We are a small company with a close-knit team looking to hire an appointment setter/recruiter with a friendly personality to join our team. We view all members of the company as highly valued team players, working to grow the company together.



As a recruiter, you will be posting job ads, sourcing, communicating with applicants, screening, qualifying, and scheduling virtual interviews with candidates for open caregiver positions. We build relationships with our caregivers with the very first phone call- which is you! You will have a lot of interaction over the phone, Instant messaging, and
 with our HR Coordinator for the recruiting responsibilities. There will be goals and quotas for recruiting and appointment setting.



WORK HOURS:

This is a Part-Time position. Work hours will be from 9AM - 1PM Pacific Standard Time. 



JOB DUTIES:

• Post and/or refresh multiple job postings

• Source candidates from Indeed and multiple other job sites, past applicant database (we have over 5,000!)

• Call,
, and/or text applicants to build rapport, screen & qualify, and schedule virtual interviews

• Remind applicants of interview and reschedule if needed

• Contact missed interview candidates to reschedule interviews daily

• Contact dormant applicants & recruit from that group daily

• Assist applicants in applying for their HCA registry & LIVESCAN background checks.

• Book interviews with qualified applicants & notify team (HR or team) via phone / instant messaging / calendar

• Conference Call in for daily stand-up meetings (8:30am & 4:30pm)

• Analyze data from recruiting metrics and make adjustments accordingly to recruiting attempts (posting ads, sourcing, rapport building, or other factors that affect relationship with applicants)

• Stay up to date on recruiting trends & apply knowledge towards role

• Research competitors and adjust recruiting techniques so we remain competitive



• GOAL to hire 10-15 per month



DAILY (minimum- company will adjust goals if needed):

• Source: 30 candidates

• Schedule Interviews: 4-6/day Mon-Friday

• Contact & screen 50 potential (determine if they are actively looking, pending HCA, or dormant.) Build rapport & invite to interview if qualified.

title: Bookkeeper & Operations Support
description:
STRbkeep is a bookkeeping practice built specifically for short-term rental (STR) property owners and small hospitality businesses. We currently manage the books for 45+ clients, from individual property owners to multi-property LLCs, and we're growing.



We're not just maintaining books. We're actively building the systems, workflows, and standards that will define how this practice operates as it scales. That means our processes are evolving, we're testing new technology, and we're looking for someone who's excited by that, not intimidated by it.



This is not a heads-down, task-list-only position. Yes, there is real bookkeeping to do: reconciliations, categorizing transactions, recording platform fees. But the day-to-day bookkeeping here is straightforward. What matters more is how you think, how you communicate, and how you approach your work.



Here's what that looks like in practice: You open a client's QBO file for the first time. You look through the chart of accounts, the bank connections, how transactions have been categorized. You form an understanding of how things are set up, and then you come to me with questions. Not just "What do I do?" but "I see it's set up this way. Is that intentional? Here's what I'd suggest."



If you notice three clients have slightly different names for the same account, or that a bank rule could save 20 minutes a month, or that our work-tracking template is missing a column, we want to hear it. You'll have a direct line to the owner, and your input will shape how we build this practice.



I'll be honest: I value curiosity, coachability, and clear communication over years of bookkeeping experience. I can teach someone the technical side of this work. I can't teach someone to care, to think critically, or to speak up when something doesn't look right.



What You'll Do:

Bookkeeping

- Record bank and credit card activity in QuickBooks Online

- Reconcile bank accounts and credit cards on a monthly basis

- Categorize and reclassify transactions; review P&L reports for misclassified expenses

- Split mortgage payments, Stripe payouts, and platform fees across appropriate accounts

- Record STR platform fees (Airbnb, VRBO, etc.) in QBO

- Assist with catch-up bookkeeping for clients who are behind

- Support year-end work including 1099 review and preparation



Administrative & Operations Support

- Follow up with clients to collect statements, documents, and access credentials

- Help onboard new clients: QBO setup, connecting bank feeds, initial data entry

- Track task completion and flag items that need the owner's review

- Help maintain and update standardized systems across 45+ client accounts (bank rules, chart of accounts naming, client reference data)

- Assist with documenting processes so the practice can scale beyond one person's memory



Systems & Process Improvement

- Review how each client's QBO file is set up and ask questions to understand the reasoning

- Identify inconsistencies across client files and suggest standardization

- Be open to testing new tools and technology as we evaluate what works best

- Help build out workflows in tools like Airtable, Content Snare, and others as we grow





Position Details

- Type: Independent contractor, remote

- Hours: Full-time, 40 hours per week

- Pay: $7–$9/hr USD, based on experience and scope of work

- Schedule: Flexible — you set your own hours and deliver by agreed deadlines

- Overlap: During onboarding, we'll need 2–3 hours of overlap during US Central Time business hours for walkthroughs and Q&A. Once you're comfortable, the schedule becomes more flexible.



This is our first hire. You won't be joining a team — you'll be helping build one. That means your input matters from day one, and the role will grow as the practice grows.



Who You Are

Curious and observant. You don't just do what's in front of you — you notice patterns, ask why things are the way they are, and think about how they could be better. When you open a client's file, you're reading the setup, not just the task list.



Self-motivated. You can take a general direction and run with it. You don't need to be checked on constantly, and you're comfortable figuring things out before asking for help — but you also know when to ask.



A clear communicator. You can articulate what you're seeing in a client's books, explain your reasoning, and frame suggestions in a way that's easy to act on. You're not afraid to say "This doesn't make sense to me" or "Here's what I'd change."



Comfortable with "in progress." Our systems are being built right now. If you need a perfectly documented SOP before you can start, this isn't the right fit. If you're energized by helping create those SOPs, it is.



A team player. As we grow, you'll be working alongside other tea
mbers. We're looking for people who collaborate well, share what they learn, and genuinely want to help build something together.



Requirements

- QuickBooks Online familiarity — you should understand the basics of how QBO works (bank feeds, categorization, reconciliations). If you're still building your QBO skills but you're a fast learner, that's okay too — we'll invest in your training.

- Basic to intermediate Excel or Google Sheets skills

- Fluent written and spoken English

- Reliable computer and stable internet connection

- Dependable — meets deadlines and communicates proactively





Nice to Have

- Experience with bank reconciliations across multiple accounts

- Familiarity with Airbnb, VRBO, or other short-term rental platforms

- Experience working with US-based clients or a US-based bookkeeping/accounting firm

- QuickBooks Online certification

- Experience with workflow tools, automation, or process documentation (Airtable, Trello, Slack, etc.)

- Prior experience in a small or growing practice where you wore multiple hats





Training Provided

You don't need to know the short-term rental industry to apply. We'll walk you through our client workflows, STR-specific categorization rules, and how we approach the work. If you're comfortable with technology and can follow a process while thinking critically about what you're doing, we'll teach you the rest.



What to Expect

You'll work directly with the owner, Cheyenne. Early on, expect a collaborative onboarding period where you'll walk through client files together, discuss how things are set up and why, and establish shared expectations. Over time, the goal is for you to work more independently while continuing to surface questions and ideas.

We're also actively evaluating new tools and technology. If you're someone who likes trying new things and has opinions about what works, you'll have a voice in those decisions.



IMPORTANT — To Apply

Submit an application with your resume and a short video (2–3 minutes). Everything below must be spoken in your video — written responses will not substitute.



In your video, please:

1. Address Cheyenne by name.

2. Tell me briefly about your experience — it doesn't have to be bookkeeping. I want to hear what you've done and how you talk about your work.

3. Answer this question on camera: "Describe a time you were working through a task and realized the instructions you had were not enough to move forward correctly. What did you do?"



Applications without a video, or with a pre-recorded generic video, will not be considered.

title: SEO / Customer Service
description:
Fluker’s Cricket Farm is an insect agriculture company offering insect ingredients to the insect-consumption pet marketplace. Our mission is to contribute to a more circular food system by producing sustainable insect ingredients for pet food and pet nutrition. As Fluker’s Cricket Farm enters an exciting new stage of growth and business expansion, we are looking for an SEO and Customer Service Coordinator to join our Office Division.



SEO Duties:



* Manage and support SEO initiatives to improve organic search visibility and website performance.

* Use SEMRush and other digital tools to conduct keyword research, track rankings, monitor competitors, and identify growth opportunities.

* Assist in optimizing website content, product pages, metadata, category pages, and blogs for search engines.

* Create and maintain SEO reports covering keyword rankings, traffic trends, content opportunities, and site performance.

* Work with internal teams to identify opportunities for new content based on customer search behavior and business priorities.

* Monitor website health and flag SEO issues related to broken links, duplicate content, missing metadata, and other on-site concerns.

* Help develop content briefs and recommendations for blogs, landing pages, and product descriptions.

* Track and report on campaign performance, organic traffic, and conversion trends.

* Support ongoing improvements to site structure, internal linking, and overall search performance.



Customer Service Duties:



* Respond promptly and professionally to customer inquiries regarding products, orders, invoices, and account questions.

* Use Zendesk to manage customer service tickets, document customer interactions, track resolutions, and maintain timely follow-up.

* Build and maintain strong relationships with customers to ensure a positive experience with Fluker’s.

* Assist in resolving customer complaints by investigating issues and providing timely, practical solutions.

* Maintain accurate records of customer interactions, including resolutions and follow-up actions.

* Provide customers with clear information regarding order status, product availability, payment timelines, and outstanding balances when needed.

* Work closely with internal teams to ensure customer issues are communicated clearly and resolved efficiently.

* Help monitor customer feedback, service trends, and recurring issues to identify opportunities for improvement.



Additional Duties:



* Support the marketing, e-commerce, and accounting teams with ad hoc projects and administrative tasks as assigned.

* Assist in maintaining compliance with company policies and procedures.

* Create and maintain data-based reports on website performance, customer service trends, and related business activities.

* Contribute to improving the overall customer experience across digital and service channels.



Requirements



* 1+ years of experience in SEO, digital marketing, e-commerce, customer service, or a related role

* Experience using SEMrush

* Experience using Zendesk or similar customer service ticketing platforms preferred

* Strong written and verbal communication skills

* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

* Ability to multitask, work independently, and take ownership of projects

* Familiarity with customer service best practices and handling customer inquiries

* Experience with website content optimization, keyword research, and reporting preferred

* Knowledge of Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Shopify, BigCommerce, or similar platforms is a plus

* Knowledge of or interest in the exotic pet industry is a plus

* Willingness to work during office hours in the Central Time Zone
